method: design review matrix using PR #25 pilot placeholder/base colors and a proposed quiet combat-floor midtone; ratios are WCAG-style relative-luminance ratios used only as comparative signals, not a gameplay-text compliance claim

rules: warm sand/copper may appear at seams and perimeter
tile-center values favor quiet neutral/cool separation
avoid pilot-color patches beneath unit centers

hueContrast.risk: green costume can merge with moss
hueContrast.artMitigation: keep moss outside playable cells; use cool-neutral tile centers and warm/copper seams

luminanceContrast.risk: ratio is modest on midtone stone
luminanceContrast.artMitigation: avoid floor highlights directly behind torso/hood and preserve a darker quiet center

silhouetteReadability.risk: hood and bow can tangent with banners/arches
silhouetteReadability.artMitigation: keep vertical props outside HP/silhouette envelope and no dark lines under bow

cyanEffectReadability.risk: cool fill can mute cyan markers near green
cyanEffectReadability.artMitigation: do not paint cyan accents into the floor; keep target state gold and AoE violet

greenCostumeReadability.artMitigation: moss is sparse, desaturated and perimeter-only

shadowReadability.risk: dark cracks may merge with contact shadow
shadowReadability.artMitigation: keep unit-foot quiet area free of heavy cracks

hueContrast.risk: teal may approach cool fill in shadow
hueContrast.artMitigation: retain warm-neutral floor and reserve blue-gray for low-saturation shadows

luminanceContrast.risk: bright stone highlights can reduce separation
luminanceContrast.artMitigation: keep highlights at seams, not tile centers

silhouetteReadability.risk: small rounded body is easily lost in noisy cracks
silhouetteReadability.artMitigation: largest quiet zone around tile center and no circular rune beneath Slime

cyanEffectReadability.risk: cyan projectile/marker can merge with Slime body
cyanEffectReadability.artMitigation: no cyan board accents; pair effects with value/edge distinction and use violet AoE contract

shadowReadability.risk: small contact shadow can disappear on dark grout
shadowReadability.artMitigation: grout must not cross the central foot footprint

hueContrast.risk: tan stone body can blend with the requested warm sand/light-brown floor
hueContrast.artMitigation: put warm sand/copper at perimeter/seams; keep central floor cooler and darker than Golem

luminanceContrast.risk: lowest ratio of the three pilots
luminanceContrast.artMitigation: avoid #8a7a63-like midtones under units and maintain cool dark separation around tile centers

silhouetteReadability.risk: largest body can merge with border masonry at edge tiles
silhouetteReadability.artMitigation: side/front border remains low and darker/cooler; no same-value wall tangent

cyanEffectReadability.risk: cyan effects can be dulled by cool fill
cyanEffectReadability.artMitigation: keep cyan absent from board paint and maintain clean dark receiving area

stoneBodyReadability.risk: shared material family with Arena Ruins
stoneBodyReadability.artMitigation: differentiate with temperature, value grouping and larger low-frequency floor shapes; never recolor Golem identity

shadowReadability.risk: large dark contact shadow can merge with border/large cracks
shadowReadability.artMitigation: reserve uniform mid-dark receiving zone under each tile center

globalAcceptance: Do not recolor any pilot identity to solve contrast.
Each pilot silhouette, HP bar and contact shadow must read on center and every edge tile.
Cyan effects must remain distinct from Slime and cool fill; green Archer must remain distinct from moss; Golem must remain distinct from all stone layers.
Risks remain open until future Checkpoint 3 with actual produced board art; this matrix does not claim a pass.
